description: The default denylist for JAAS login modules only blocked JndiLoginModule. LdapLoginModule was omitted, allowing an attacker who controls a JAAS configuration (e.g., via sasl.jaas.config) to force an outbound LDAP connection to an arbitrary server, enabling SSRF or credential capture.

sources.description: The class name of a JAAS login module supplied through attacker-controllable configuration (JAAS file or sasl.jaas.config property).
sources.notes: Value ultimately comes from the JAAS config file path passed via 'java.security.auth.login.config' system property, or the inline 'sasl.jaas.config' Kafka config, both of which are operator- or attacker-controlled.

sources.description: The string literal (or static-final field value) used as the defaultValue argument to System.getProperty('org.apache.kafka.disallowed.login.modules', DEFAULT). This value defines which login modules are blocked when no override is set.
sources.notes: The vulnerable value is 'com.sun.security.auth.module.JndiLoginModule' — it omits LdapLoginModule. The fixed value appends ',com.sun.security.auth.module.LdapLoginModule'.

sinks.description: The Set<String>.contains() call that tests whether the login module name is on the denylist. If the Set was built from an incomplete default, this check is the point where the bypass occurs.
sinks.notes: The security guarantee is: if contains() returns true, an IllegalArgumentException is thrown, blocking the module. A false-negative here allows a dangerous module to proceed.

sanitizers.description: If a non-empty system property 'org.apache.kafka.disallowed.login.modules' is set, the default value is not used; operator-provided list is used instead. An operator who explicitly lists LdapLoginModule here is not vulnerable.
sanitizers.condition: System.getProperty(DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG, DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT) reads from the system property, not the default, when the property is set.
sanitizers.notes: CodeQL should treat the result of System.getProperty(key, default) as sanitized only when the default itself is complete OR when a taint-barrier models the non-default branch.

barrier_conditions.description: The vulnerability is absent if the default string contains 'LdapLoginModule' (case-sensitive class name check). Post-fix the default is 'com.sun.security.auth.module.JndiLoginModule,com.sun.security.auth.module.LdapLoginModule'.

primary_path.description: Incomplete default denylist flows through System.getProperty → split → trim → Set, then Set.contains(loginModuleName) returns false for LdapLoginModule, allowing the module to load and establish an outbound LDAP connection.

attacker_control_path.description: Attacker-controlled JAAS config specifies LdapLoginModule; getLoginModuleName() returns the attacker's chosen class name; it reaches the same contains() sink.

exploit_conditions: Attacker can supply or influence a JAAS config file path (java.security.auth.login.config) or the sasl.jaas.config Kafka property.
The default system property 'org.apache.kafka.disallowed.login.modules' is in use (not overridden by operator).
The Kafka version predates the fix (DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT does not include LdapLoginModule).

fix_pattern.description: Add 'com.sun.security.auth.module.LdapLoginModule' to the DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT constant so the security check blocks it even without operator configuration.

key_predicates: isIncompleteDefaultDenylist(StringLiteral lit): lit is the second arg of System.getProperty(DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG, lit) and not lit.getValue().matches("%LdapLoginModule%")
isDenylistContainsCheck(MethodAccess ma): ma calls Set.contains() inside throwIfLoginModuleIsNotAllowed or equivalent
denylistFlowsToCheck(StringLiteral src, MethodAccess sink): DataFlow path from src → Set qualifier at sink
moduleNameFlowsToCheck(MethodAccess getModName, MethodAccess containsCheck): TaintTracking from getModName return → containsCheck argument(0)
